rationale: In the citing paper, Ding, Grewal, and Liechty (2005) appears only as part of a grouped citation in the conclusion noting that incentive-aligned mechanisms have shown potential in conjoint research. The paper does not build its ASE method, hypotheses, estimation, or empirical tests on that cited study, and the main comparisons are against ACA, FPM, and SEM. There is no distinct functional use of the 2005 paper beyond brief background context for a future-research point.
